steps.title: Check whether the equations are proportional
steps.content: Multiply Equation 1 by 3: $$3 \times (0.5x + 1.2y) = 3 \times 9 \implies 1.5x + 3.6y = 27$$ This is exactly Equation 2, so both equations represent the same line.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Infinitely many': Both equations reduce to the same line, so every point on that line is a solution. There are infinitely many solutions. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Exactly two': A linear system in two variables can never have exactly two solutions — it has zero, one, or infinitely many. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Exactly one': One solution requires two distinct lines with different slopes that intersect at a single point. Since the two equations are identical (same slope, same intercept), there is no unique crossing point. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Zero': Zero solutions requires parallel lines with the same slope but different y-intercepts. These equations have the same slope AND the same intercept — they are identical, not parallel non-intersecting lines. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: All three coefficient ratios equal 3: $$\frac{1.5}{0.5} = \frac{3.6}{1.2} = \frac{27}{9} = 3$$ The equations are equivalent, giving infinitely many solutions. That matches choice A — A is correct.
